DeletionProtection.description: A parameter to configure deletion protection. Deletion protection prevents a user from deleting a configuration profile or an environment if AppConfig has called either GetLatestConfiguration or for the configuration profile or from the environment during the specified interval. The default interval for ProtectionPeriodInMinutes is 60.

Enabled.description: A parameter that indicates if deletion protection is enabled or not.

ProtectionPeriodInMinutes.description: The time interval during which AppConfig monitors for calls to GetLatestConfiguration or for a configuration profile or from an environment. AppConfig returns an error if a user calls or for the designated configuration profile or environment. To bypass the error and delete a configuration profile or an environment, specify BYPASS for the DeletionProtectionCheck parameter for either or .

NextToken.description: The token for the next set of items to return. Use this token to get the next set of results.
The token for the next set of items to return. Use this token to get the next set of results.

DeploymentDurationInMinutes.description: Total amount of time the deployment lasted.

GrowthType.description: The algorithm used to define how percentage grew over time.

GrowthFactor.description: The percentage of targets that received a deployed configuration during each interval.

FinalBakeTimeInMinutes.description: The amount of time that AppConfig monitored for alarms before considering the deployment to be complete and no longer eligible for automatic rollback.

ReplicateTo.description: Save the deployment strategy to a Systems Manager (SSM) document.

Items.description: The list of extension associations. Each item represents an extension association to an application, environment, or configuration profile.

NextToken.description: The token for the next set of items to return. Use this token to get the next set of results.

Id.description: The extension association ID. This ID is used to call other ExtensionAssociation API actions such as GetExtensionAssociation or DeleteExtensionAssociation.

ExtensionArn.description: The system-generated Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the extension.

ResourceArn.description: The ARNs of applications, configuration profiles, or environments defined in the association.

Items.description: The list of available extensions. The list includes Amazon Web Services authored and user-created extensions.

NextToken.description: The token for the next set of items to return. Use this token to get the next set of results.

Arn.description: The system-generated Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the extension.

DeletionProtection.description: A parameter to configure deletion protection. Deletion protection prevents a user from deleting a configuration profile or an environment if AppConfig has called either GetLatestConfiguration or for the configuration profile or from the environment during the specified interval. The default interval for ProtectionPeriodInMinutes is 60.

Enabled.description: A parameter that indicates if deletion protection is enabled or not.

ProtectionPeriodInMinutes.description: The time interval during which AppConfig monitors for calls to GetLatestConfiguration or for a configuration profile or from an environment. AppConfig returns an error if a user calls or for the designated configuration profile or environment. To bypass the error and delete a configuration profile or an environment, specify BYPASS for the DeletionProtectionCheck parameter for either or .

RetrievalRoleArn.description: The ARN of an IAM role with permission to access the configuration at the specified LocationUri.

Validators.description: A list of methods for validating the configuration.

Type.description:
The type of configurations contained in the profile. AppConfig supports feature flags and freeform configurations. We recommend you create feature flag configurations to enable or disable new features and freeform configurations to distribute configurations to an application. When calling this API, enter one of the following values for Type:

AWS.AppConfig.FeatureFlags

AWS.Freeform

KmsKeyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name of the Key Management Service key to encrypt new configuration data versions in the AppConfig hosted configuration store. This attribute is only used for hosted configuration types. To encrypt data managed in other configuration stores, see the documentation for how to specify an KMS key for that particular service.

KmsKeyIdentifier.description: The Key Management Service key identifier (key ID, key alias, or key ARN) provided when the resource was created or updated.

Content.description: Either the JSON Schema content or the Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of an Lambda function.

GrowthType.description: The algorithm used to define how percentage grew over time.

GrowthFactor.description: The percentage of targets that received a deployed configuration during each interval.

FinalBakeTimeInMinutes.description: The amount of time that AppConfig monitored for alarms before considering the deployment to be complete and no longer eligible for automatic rollback.

ReplicateTo.description: Save the deployment strategy to a Systems Manager (SSM) document.

State.description: The state of the environment. An environment can be in one of the following states: READY_FOR_DEPLOYMENT, DEPLOYING, ROLLING_BACK, or ROLLED_BACK

Monitors.description: Amazon CloudWatch alarms monitored during the deployment.

AlarmArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Amazon CloudWatch alarm.

AlarmRoleArn.description: ARN of an Identity and Access Management (IAM) role for AppConfig to monitor AlarmArn.

Arn.description: The system-generated Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the extension.

Parameters.description: The parameters accepted by the extension. You specify parameter values when you associate the extension to an AppConfig resource by using the CreateExtensionAssociation API action. For Lambda extension actions, these parameters are included in the Lambda request object.

ExtensionArn.description: The ARN of the extension defined in the association.

ResourceArn.description: The ARNs of applications, configuration profiles, or environments defined in the association.

Parameters.description: The parameter names and values defined in the association.

ExtensionVersionNumber.description: The version number for the extension defined in the association.

Content.description: The content of the configuration or the configuration data.

ContentType.description: A standard MIME type describing the format of the configuration content. For more information, see Content-Type.

VersionLabel.description: A user-defined label for an AppConfig hosted configuration version.

KmsKeyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name of the Key Management Service key that was used to encrypt this specific version of the configuration data in the AppConfig hosted configuration store.

Content.description:
The content of the configuration or the configuration data.

The Content attribute only contains data if the system finds new or updated configuration data. If there is no new or updated data and ClientConfigurationVersion matches the version of the current configuration, AppConfig returns a 204 No Content HTTP response code and the Content value will be empty.

ContentType.description: A standard MIME type describing the format of the configuration content. For more information, see Content-Type.

When calling this API, enter one of the following values for Type:

AWS.AppConfig.FeatureFlags

AWS.Freeform

KmsKeyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name of the Key Management Service key to encrypt new configuration data versions in the AppConfig hosted configuration store. This attribute is only used for hosted configuration types. To encrypt data managed in other configuration stores, see the documentation for how to specify an KMS key for that particular service.

KmsKeyIdentifier.description: The Key Management Service key identifier (key ID, key alias, or key ARN) provided when the resource was created or updated.

Content.description: Either the JSON Schema content or the Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of an Lambda function.

ApplicationId.description: The ID of the application that was deployed.

EnvironmentId.description: The ID of the environment that was deployed.

DeploymentStrategyId.description: The ID of the deployment strategy that was deployed.

ConfigurationProfileId.description: The ID of the configuration profile that was deployed.

ConfigurationLocationUri.description: Information about the source location of the configuration.

DeploymentDurationInMinutes.description: Total amount of time the deployment lasted.

GrowthType.description: The algorithm used to define how percentage grew over time.

GrowthFactor.description: The percentage of targets to receive a deployed configuration during each interval.

FinalBakeTimeInMinutes.description: The amount of time that AppConfig monitored for alarms before considering the deployment to be complete and no longer eligible for automatic rollback.

EventLog.description: A list containing all events related to a deployment. The most recent events are displayed first.

PercentageComplete.description: The percentage of targets for which the deployment is available.

AppliedExtensions.description: A list of extensions that were processed as part of the deployment. The extensions that were previously associated to the configuration profile, environment, or the application when StartDeployment was called.

KmsKeyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name of the Key Management Service key used to encrypt configuration data. You can encrypt secrets stored in Secrets Manager, Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) objects encrypted with SSE-KMS, or secure string parameters stored in Amazon Web Services Systems Manager Parameter Store.

KmsKeyIdentifier.description: The Key Management Service key identifier (key ID, key alias, or key ARN) provided when the resource was created or updated.

VersionLabel.description: A user-defined label for an AppConfig hosted configuration version.

EventType.description: The type of deployment event. Deployment event types include the start, stop, or completion of a deployment; a percentage update; the start or stop of a bake period; and the start or completion of a rollback.

TriggeredBy.description: The entity that triggered the deployment event. Events can be triggered by a user, AppConfig, an Amazon CloudWatch alarm, or an internal error.

Description.description:
A description of the deployment event. Descriptions include, but are not limited to, the following:

  • The Amazon Web Services account or the Amazon CloudWatch alarm ARN that initiated a rollback.

  • The percentage of hosts that received the deployment.

  • A recommendation to attempt a new deployment (in the case of an internal error).

ActionInvocations.description: The list of extensions that were invoked as part of the deployment.

OccurredAt.description: The date and time the event occurred.

DeploymentDurationInMinutes.description: Total amount of time the deployment lasted.

GrowthType.description: The algorithm used to define how percentage grew over time.

GrowthFactor.description: The percentage of targets that received a deployed configuration during each interval.

FinalBakeTimeInMinutes.description: The amount of time that AppConfig monitored for alarms before considering the deployment to be complete and no longer eligible for automatic rollback.

ReplicateTo.description: Save the deployment strategy to a Systems Manager (SSM) document.
